title: Mandevilla glandulosa
taxonomy: Mandevilla glandulosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to South America and is a member of the genus Mandevilla. Mandevilla glandulosa is a climbing plant with showy, fragrant flowers and is often grown as an ornamental plant. The classification of Mandevilla glandulosa also includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Gentianales. Mandevilla glandulosa is a unique and beautiful addition to any garden or indoor space.
uses: Mandevilla glandulosa is a plant species native to Central and South America, and research suggests it may have various medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Additionally, some studies indicate that Mandevilla glandulosa may exhibit antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. The plant's extracts are also being explored for their potential in traditional medicine.

side_effects: Mandevilla glandulosa, a plant known for its beautiful flowers and climbing nature, can cause allergic reactions or skin irritation in some individuals due to the sap it produces. The sap may contain allergens that can trigger reactions such as redness, itching, or swelling. Ingesting any part of the plant can be toxic, causing symptoms like nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. If ingested in large quantities, it may lead to more severe health issues, including liver or kidney damage. It's essential to handle the plant carefully and avoid ingesting any of its parts to avoid potential side effects.
